title: Level 304

Race Day
 ----
[[File:Indoortrack1.png|center|link=|750x750px]]
''"High knees"'',

I say to myself. I slowly go through my warmups on this suspiciously empty track. I run my race in approximately 27 minutes. There is no time to waste, this is it.

''"A-skips"''. [[File:Indoortrack2.png|center|link=|750x750px]]
Me, myself, and the 60m of grubby, indoor track.

''"High kicks"''.

My race approaches rapidly. Calls go by like lightning, yet there are still no people. I'm all alone with 200 meters of echoes and liminality. A subtle thud reverberates in this empty room every time I take a step.

''"Karaokes."'' [[File:Indoortrack3.png|center|link=|750x750px]]
''"Back pedals"'',

I continue, breathing picked up, along with my pace. My event should be called any minute now. I got so used to hearing that stupid automated voice calling every event that I guess I didn't notice when it went quiet.

''"B-skips"''.

The top floor is just as empty as the bottom. Maybe I should go out there and see what event they're on. ----
'''Leave the Indoor Track?'''
'''Leave the Outdoor Track?'''

{{Class Custom Image|hexagon=4|class=4|safety=Unsafe|security=Unstable|entity=Unconfirmed Entity Count|bg-image=305hurdles.png}} {{Background|file=305hurdles.png}}
Description

Level 304 consists of a 400-meter athletic track comprised of polyurethane and synthetic rubber. Painted on this track are lines representing starting and ending points, including race waterfalls and hurdle markings. There are hurdles scattered throughout the level, but, likely due to the state of misuse the track is in, they are broken, rusted, or covered in vegetation around the outskirts of the track. The track is surrounded by overgrown grass, weeds, and a dense, extensive forest that continues in most directions for an inconclusive, and likely innumerable, distance.

In the middle of the track, there is a football field with yard lines painted on it. The center of the field—where a mascot or team name would usually be—is completely blank. There are field goals and scoreboards set up but they aren’t usually functional, except for some instances at night. [[File:305hurdles.png|thumb|250px|An image of stacked hurdles found near the 800m waterfall line.]] In instances occurring in no known pattern, a “phantom” football game will occur. It is common for some of the floodlights to turn on at varying and incomprehensible intervals during the night—on these phantom nights, however, they will all turn on, alongside the scoreboards. There is no determined outcome for these games—they are random and last as long as the night does. Scores add up in ways similar to football games, corresponding with the number of points that touchdowns, safeties, and other methods of scoring would provide. Despite this, nothing is happening on the field—giving the wistful vibe that this field, which has fallen into disuse, still reminisces of the days it was played on. The day/night cycle of Level 304 is sporadic and unpredictable. Night and day change almost instantaneously, with survivability throughout the level adjusting according to the time of day. The forest becomes especially hazardous—it is incredibly easy to become disoriented and be led astray from the field. Entity encounters in the forest are rare—still, it’s unadvised to enter unless necessary due to the unpredictable nature of time changes. Despite these warnings, wanderers and explorers who have found their way back (primarily due to their inventory of equipment—do not enter the forest if not properly equipped) have documented the existence of a creek. There have been no signs of life in the creek itself—excluding bacterial life forms such as the [[Hydrolitis Plague]]—though the forest is an image of direct contrast. Birds have not been sighted, though the ambiance of their songs is unavoidable, even on the track and field. In one corner of the track is a wide dirt pathway that leads to a high school building. Despite the apparently active nature of high schools—filled with conversation, people, and endless chatter—it is similarly surrounded by the forest, maintaining the same innumerous density, and is devoid of life. The building contains all the facilities that a common American high school would have, with one apparent difference—the lack of any and all fixtures. Like the football field, there is no name of the school, nor are there any mascots on the walls; there are no posters, no drawings, nothing. The facility is completely blank, devoid of any creative substance. While the track is in a state of disuse, the high school remains completely spotless and with no need for repairs. Dust does not seem to collect in the building. Despite the hospitality and cleanliness of the high school, it is completely devoid of life. Explorations of the exterior of the building have revealed that it is quite extensive, with a separate facility of three stories several hundred yards away from the rest of the premises. Additionally, at the edge of an empty parking lot, past barren tennis courts (though, in accordance with the remainder of the facilities, clean and uncracked), is a sign that reads “Welcome to.” The sign is the furthest part of the establishment known to exist—the forest border, maintaining an eerie smogginess, begins just a few paces past it. Correlations between the bereft track and the spotless high school are few and far between—the prerequisite for many theories regarding the level’s nature. Questions regarding the uneasy contrast between the two maintain intrigue—and mystery. Why the track’s entire ambiance appears so submerged in nature, and the high school, in direct contrast, so uniformly, eerily tidy, is completely unknown—how can they be a part of the same facility, yet differ so much in terms of maintenance? Entering the high school facilities is also unadvised, without proper equipment. The complete lack of natural light conveys a palpable eeriness—additionally, the once luminous, finite facilities develop labyrinthine qualities. This can be combated by utilizing equipment capable of producing light—even a slight amount of light can save a wanderer’s life. It is unknown whether this trait genuinely influences the composition of the facilities, though accounts tend to highly point in that direction. Nights spent in a lightless high school are horrid—the now possibly limitless, entangled mazes of halls are completely devoid of light and perpetuate the belief that you are not alone—many times, this culminates in a wanderer’s death. While initially believed to be the blatant terror of the situation, many researchers now believe that nights spent in the high school last longer than those spent elsewhere. The observable longevity of such nights concludes with a gradual switch to daylight—contrasting with the instantaneous nature of the day-night cycle. The means by which these longer nights are achieved baffle researchers, who have come to no reasonable conclusion. There are many theories regarding the odd phenomenon, though none are agreed upon. Attempting to measure these nights has also seen minuscule results. Digital watches simply stop functioning if a wanderer is in the high school at night with no equipment capable of producing light. Hand and pocket watches cannot be observed due to the lack of light—nevertheless, if a wanderer survives, the time on the watch is the exact same as it was beforehand. One wanderer described the night he spent as “absolute hell” and noted that his watch had stopped at 5:02 P.M. when it became nighttime. Despite his description of the night as “feeling like several days,” he came out with his watch still at 5:02 P.M. It is the consensus of the vast majority of researchers that the high school facilities do, in fact, enlarge, during nights with or without equipment. This is not observable from the outside—the relative dimension in space appears to change, making the high school bigger on the inside. Entities have been documented in the facilities, though they are not documented to appear when wanderers are well-equipped. Unequipped wanderers traveling in groups are picked off one by one—however, there tends to be a survivor in these cases, which serves as the only reliable sources regarding entity habitation.

Colonies and Outposts

Colonies and outposts are unable to be established in '''Level 304'''. This is because of the constantly fluctuating properties, architecture, and safety of the level.

Entities

Only one entity is confirmed to inhabit Level 304. There have been sightings of other entities, but this is the only one that is likely to maintain inhabitance in the level for an extensive period of time. [[Hydrolitis Plague|Hydrolitis Plague]] Found in reservoirs that are located away from the rest of the creek, the Hydrolitis Plague thrives in stagnant water which maintains a high insect presence. Though instances of the plague are rare, it is confirmed to exist in Level 304. The creek, however, is of little concern—the plague only flourishes in locations containing stagnant water, and the presence of insects worsens the matter. Entering the creek should be of minimal concern unless these factors are true—still, taking precautions is highly recommended, as intensive care is nearly impossible in Level 304, and the disease has a mortality rate of 84%. Undocumented Entities
These entities, not given a name due to lack of characteristical information, inhabit the high school facilities during the night. They are known to stay away from wanderers who are well-equipped; only unequipped wanderers are subdued by them. The pitch blackness—alongside the fact that these entities appear only to people with minimal equipment—has led to barely any conclusive evidence being found. They are assumed to be quick and may use the psychosomatic feeling of being watched that is omnipresent within the high school.

Entrances and Exits
Entrances
Those who have found themselves in '''Level 304''' have no recollection of doing so. It has been theorized that they come from levels based on the terrain surrounding the track such as the lake and ongoing forests, but this has been deemed impossible to identify due to the mass amounts of levels of these origins. Further research is being conducted on this phenomena.
Exits
Similar to the theorized entrance to '''Level 304''', output levels are typically forested or contain freshwater bodies of water. The unidentifiable nature of entrances and exits has no known source, though researchers are still studying the level's various anomalies.
''Forest-based''
Levels under this category are forest-inhabited, meaning that '''Level 304''' Is only accessible by forest-like portions of each level.
-[[Level 45]]
-[[Level 173]] (This level has both freshwater bodies and forests. '''Level 304''' is accessible by either.
''Water-based''
Levels under this category are water-inhabited, meaning that '''Level 304''' Is only accessible by water-like portions of each level.
-[[Level 37]]
